note: This list grows forever and is never pruned. Every phrase here caused a negative lead reaction.

phrases.why: Meaningless without evidence. Leads who need reassurance need proof, not appeals to trust.
phrases.what_happened: Triggers disengagement in certainty-seekers and betrayal-damaged leads
phrases.alternative: Here's what happened when [comparable situation]: [specific outcome]

phrases.why: Vague positive claim. Feels like a sales line.
phrases.what_happened: Analytical leads immediately probe for specifics — if you can't follow up with numbers, you've weakened your credibility
phrases.alternative: Based on comparable properties, here's a realistic range: [floor]–[ceiling]/month

phrases.phrase: we never have problems with guests
phrases.why: Statistically implausible. Leads who have done their research know damage happens on all platforms.
phrases.what_happened: Betrayal-damaged and certainty-seeking leads immediately distrust any absolutist claim
phrases.alternative: We have a clear process when things go wrong — here's what it looks like: [specific steps]

phrases.why: 'Only' minimises the concern rather than addressing it. Leads hear 'I don't take your concern seriously.'
phrases.what_happened: Invites defensiveness from analytical leads who want the calculation, not the dismissal
phrases.alternative: At 13%, on your projected £X, here's the exact net you'd receive: £Y

phrases.phrase: don't worry about the quiet months
phrases.why: Dismisses a legitimate concern without addressing it. Income consistency is the #1 recurring worry.
phrases.what_happened: Leads who raised this concern and heard 'don't worry' consistently came back to it later — or abandoned
phrases.alternative: Let me show you what your worst month looks like. Even in [quietest month], properties like yours earn £X.

phrases.why: Misleading. Owners have responsibilities (mortgage, insurance, council tax, gas safety). Over-promising passivity creates post-conversion disappointment.
phrases.what_happened: Leads who expected zero involvement became difficult clients when their responsibilities emerged
phrases.alternative: Your responsibilities are: [specific list]. Everything else is us. Most owners tell us it genuinely feels passive once you're set up.

phrases.why: Vague claim without specifics. 'Vetted' means different things to different leads.
phrases.what_happened: Guest-concern leads (particularly Sam Corbett-type healthcare professionals) want to know the specific vetting criteria, not a general claim
phrases.alternative: We require a minimum of [N] reviews, verified ID, and we hold a £[X] security deposit. We can also block instant-book for your property.
